title: Boat Rental in Thessaloniki: 2026 Guide to the Thermaic Gulf
html:
To experience Thessaloniki from the water, hire a vessel from one of the city's marinas and cruise the Thermaic Gulf. A half-day motorboat rental typically starts from €250 for a group of six, and you can book a boat online through BoatHire24, which connects renters with verified owners.

Where to set off in Thessaloniki

Set off from one of several marinas and harbours that dot the Thessaloniki waterfront. The main departure points are Marina Thessaloniki (the city's largest), the small harbour at Karabournaki, and the port of Nea Moudania in the nearby Chalkidiki peninsula. Each offers easy access to the open gulf. You can book a boat online in a few minutes.

For a shorter trip, anchor in the bay of Peraia or explore the coast towards Angelochori. Further out, the islands of the Thermaic Gulf, such as Epanomi and Kassandra, are reachable within a couple of hours. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 for hire.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motorboat (6, 8 guests) Best for half-day coastal cruising; from €250.
  2. Sailing yacht (8, 10 guests) Ideal for a full-day island hop; from €600.
  3. Catamaran (10, 12 guests) Stable and spacious for groups; from €800.
  4. Motor yacht (10, 14 guests) Luxury day or overnight charter; from €1,500. Consider the Princess F70 for hire, a 70-foot flybridge model available on BoatHire24.
  5. RIB (4, 6 guests) Fast and fun for watersports; from €200.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Thessaloniki?

Prices vary by vessel type and duration, with shared tours starting at €50 per person and private charters from €200 for two hours.

  • Shared sunset tour: €50, €80 per person, 2, 3 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €250, €400 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €800, €1,200 for up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day: €1,500, €3,000 for up to 14 guests.

You can compare prices and book directly through BoatHire24, where many rentals include fuel and skipper. The platform details how boat rental works in a simple step-by-step guide. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Thessaloniki?

In Greece, a boat licence is required for bareboat rentals of vessels over 30 horsepower, but many operators offer skippered charters so you do not need one. The operator confirms exact requirements when you book. For a skipper-free experience, smaller RIBs and motorboats under 30 hp are exempt. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

A local tip: in July and August the melterni wind can pick up in the afternoon, so plan morning trips for calmer seas.

When to go

The high season runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive months for boat rental. Spring and autumn offer milder weather and lower prices, though the sea remains warm until October. By boating in May or September, you avoid the peak crowds while still enjoying long days. In 2026, Easter falls in early April, which can be a pleasant time for a quiet cruise.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50, €3,000
  • Peak months: July, August
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 4, 14 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Thessaloniki

Can I rent a boat in Thessaloniki without a licence?

Yes, you can, by choosing a skippered charter or a boat under 30 hp that does not require a licence.

What is the best island to visit from Thessaloniki?

Kassandra, the first peninsula of Chalkidiki, is a popular day destination, about 20 nautical miles from the city marina.

How far is it to Sani Marina by boat from Thessaloniki?

Sani Marina is roughly 40 nautical miles from Thessaloniki, a comfortable 2, 3 hour cruise depending on your boat's speed.

Are there any all-inclusive boat tours in Thessaloniki?

Many operators offer all-inclusive packages with fuel, skipper, snorkelling gear, and lunch, typically starting from €80 per person.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Thessaloniki or list your own. To start planning your 2026 trip, browse available vessels and check availability on the platform. If you own a boat in Thessaloniki, you can list your boat on BoatHire24 and earn extra income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Thessaloniki and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
